elements.short: A description of when an event can occur
elements.definition: The EventDefinition resource provides a reusable description of when a particular event can occur.

elements.short: Logical id of this artifact
elements.definition: The logical id of the resource, as used in the URL for the resource. Once assigned, this value never changes.

elements.short: Metadata about the resource
elements.definition: The metadata about the resource. This is content that is maintained by the infrastructure. Changes to the content might not always be associated with version changes to the resource.

elements.short: A set of rules under which this content was created
elements.definition: A reference to a set of rules that were followed when the resource was constructed, and which must be understood when processing the content. Often, this is a reference to an implementation guide that defines the special rules along with other profiles etc.

elements.short: Language of the resource content
elements.definition: The base language in which the resource is written.

elements.short: Text summary of the resource, for human interpretation
elements.definition: A human-readable narrative that contains a summary of the resource and can be used to represent the content of the resource to a human. The narrative need not encode all the structured data, but is required to contain sufficient detail to make it "clinically safe" for a human to just read the narrative. Resource definitions may define what content should be represented in the narrative to ensure clinical safety.

elements.short: Contained, inline Resources
elements.definition: These resources do not have an independent existence apart from the resource that contains them - they cannot be identified independently, and nor can they have their own independent transaction scope.

elements.short: Additional content defined by implementations
elements.definition: May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the resource. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance  applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer can define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension.

elements.short: Extensions that cannot be ignored
elements.definition:
May be used to represent additional information that is not part of the basic definition of the resource and that modifies the understanding of the element that contains it and/or the understanding of the containing element's descendants. Usually modifier elements provide negation or qualification. To make the use of extensions safe and manageable, there is a strict set of governance applied to the definition and use of extensions. Though any implementer is allowed to define an extension, there is a set of requirements that SHALL be met as part of the definition of the extension. Applications processing a resource are required to check for modifier extensions.

Modifier extensions SHALL NOT change the meaning of any elements on Resource or DomainResource (including cannot change the meaning of modifierExtension itself).

elements.short: Canonical identifier for this event definition, represented as a URI (globally unique)
elements.definition: An absolute URI that is used to identify this event definition when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance; also called its canonical identifier. This SHOULD be globally unique and SHOULD be a literal address at which at which an authoritative instance of this event definition is (or will be) published. This URL can be the target of a canonical reference. It SHALL remain the same when the event definition is stored on different servers.

elements.short: Additional identifier for the event definition
elements.definition: A formal identifier that is used to identify this event definition when it is represented in other formats, or referenced in a specification, model, design or an instance.

elements.short: Business version of the event definition
elements.definition: The identifier that is used to identify this version of the event definition when it is referenced in a specification, model, design or instance. This is an arbitrary value managed by the event definition author and is not expected to be globally unique. For example, it might be a timestamp (e.g. yyyymmdd) if a managed version is not available. There is also no expectation that versions can be placed in a lexicographical sequence.

elements.short: Name for this event definition (computer friendly)
elements.definition: A natural language name identifying the event definition. This name should be usable as an identifier for the module by machine processing applications such as code generation.

elements.short: Name for this event definition (human friendly)
elements.definition: A short, descriptive, user-friendly title for the event definition.

elements.short: Subordinate title of the event definition
elements.definition: An explanatory or alternate title for the event definition giving additional information about its content.

elements.short: draft | active | retired | unknown
elements.definition: The status of this event definition. Enables tracking the life-cycle of the content.

elements.short: For testing purposes, not real usage
elements.definition: A Boolean value to indicate that this event definition is authored for testing purposes (or education/evaluation/marketing) and is not intended to be used for genuine usage.

elements.short: Type of individual the event definition is focused on
elements.definition: A code or group definition that describes the intended subject of the event definition.

elements.short: Date last changed
elements.definition: The date  (and optionally time) when the event definition was published. The date must change when the business version changes and it must change if the status code changes. In addition, it should change when the substantive content of the event definition changes.

elements.short: Name of the publisher (organization or individual)
elements.definition: The name of the organization or individual that published the event definition.

elements.short: Contact details for the publisher
elements.definition: Contact details to assist a user in finding and communicating with the publisher.

elements.short: Natural language description of the event definition
elements.definition: A free text natural language description of the event definition from a consumer's perspective.

elements.short: The context that the content is intended to support
elements.definition: The content was developed with a focus and intent of supporting the contexts that are listed. These contexts may be general categories (gender, age, ...) or may be references to specific programs (insurance plans, studies, ...) and may be used to assist with indexing and searching for appropriate event definition instances.

elements.short: Intended jurisdiction for event definition (if applicable)
elements.definition: A legal or geographic region in which the event definition is intended to be used.

elements.short: Why this event definition is defined
elements.definition: Explanation of why this event definition is needed and why it has been designed as it has.

elements.short: Describes the clinical usage of the event definition
elements.definition: A detailed description of how the event definition is used from a clinical perspective.

elements.short: Use and/or publishing restrictions
elements.definition: A copyright statement relating to the event definition and/or its contents. Copyright statements are generally legal restrictions on the use and publishing of the event definition.

elements.short: When the event definition was approved by publisher
elements.definition: The date on which the resource content was approved by the publisher. Approval happens once when the content is officially approved for usage.

elements.short: When the event definition was last reviewed
elements.definition: The date on which the resource content was last reviewed. Review happens periodically after approval but does not change the original approval date.

elements.short: When the event definition is expected to be used
elements.definition: The period during which the event definition content was or is planned to be in active use.

elements.short: E.g. Education, Treatment, Assessment, etc.
elements.definition: Descriptive topics related to the module. Topics provide a high-level categorization of the module that can be useful for filtering and searching.

elements.short: Who authored the content
elements.definition: An individiual or organization primarily involved in the creation and maintenance of the content.

elements.short: Who edited the content
elements.definition: An individual or organization primarily responsible for internal coherence of the content.

elements.short: Who reviewed the content
elements.definition: An individual or organization primarily responsible for review of some aspect of the content.

elements.short: Who endorsed the content
elements.definition: An individual or organization responsible for officially endorsing the content for use in some setting.

elements.short: Additional documentation, citations, etc.
elements.definition: Related resources such as additional documentation, justification, or bibliographic references.

elements.short: "when" the event occurs (multiple = 'or')
elements.definition: The trigger element defines when the event occurs. If more than one trigger condition is specified, the event fires whenever any one of the trigger conditions is met.

description: The EventDefinition resource provides a reusable description of when a particular event can occur.
